channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: A humanoid robot sprints past the human half-marathon world record in Beijing race
item.description: The winner from Honor, a Chinese smartphone maker, completed the race in 50 minutes and 26 seconds, running faster than the human world record in a show of China's technological leaps.
item.content:encoded:
The winner from Honor, a Chinese smartphone maker, completed the race in 50 minutes and 26 seconds, running faster than the human world record in a show of China's technological leaps.

(Image credit: Ng Han Guan)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: U.K police investigating if attacks in London are the work of Iranian proxies
item.description: U.K. police said Sunday they are investigating whether a string of arson attacks on Jewish sites in London are the work of Iranian proxies, the latest of which caused minor damage on Saturday night.
item.content:encoded:
U.K. police said Sunday they are investigating whether a string of arson attacks on Jewish sites in London are the work of Iranian proxies, the latest of which caused minor damage on Saturday night.

(Image credit: Jamie Lashmar)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Communities launch cleanup after severe weather and tornadoes churn across Midwest
item.description: At least 66 tornado reports were submitted across multiple states on Friday, according to the National Weather Service.
item.content:encoded:
At least 66 tornado reports were submitted across multiple states on Friday, according to the National Weather Service.

(Image credit: Scott Olson)
item.dc:creator: Chandelis Duster

item.title: Will the DHS shutdown affect security for the World Cup?
item.description: The FIFA World Cup is a little over 50 days away. NPR's Rob Schmitz talks to former Department of Homeland Security official Juliette Kayyem about the security concerns people have about hosting the tournament in America in this moment.
item.content:encoded: The FIFA World Cup is a little over 50 days away. NPR's Rob Schmitz talks to former Department of Homeland Security official Juliette Kayyem about the security concerns people have about hosting the tournament in America in this moment.
item.dc:creator: Rob Schmitz

item.title: 8 children killed in a shooting in Louisiana, police say
item.description: The suspect was the father of seven of the children killed, police said. The victims ranged in age from 1 to about 14 years old, according to police in Shreveport, La. A total of 10 people were shot.
item.content:encoded:
The suspect was the father of seven of the children killed, police said. The victims ranged in age from 1 to about 14 years old, according to police in Shreveport, La. A total of 10 people were shot.

(Image credit: Gerald Herbert)
item.dc:creator: Joe Hernandez

item.title: Why nearly every farmer who grows these chile peppers is a woman
item.description: Chile peppers are a traditional part of Indian cuisine — and a key crop for women farmers. They say it's too demanding for men. "In spite of the challenges," says one, "we've found freedom."
item.content:encoded:
Chile peppers are a traditional part of Indian cuisine — and a key crop for women farmers. They say it's too demanding for men. "In spite of the challenges," says one, "we've found freedom."

(Image credit: Viraj Nayar for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Kamala Thiagarajan

item.title: U.S. seizes Iranian cargo ship in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The U.S. has taken custody of an Iranian-flagged cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz after firing on its engine room, President Trump said in a post on social media.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. has taken custody of an Iranian-flagged cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz after firing on its engine room, President Trump said in a post on social media.

(Image credit: Asghar Besharati)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Real estate investors are buying up long-term care facilities. Residents can suffer
item.description: Real estate investment trusts are landlords for thousands of nursing homes, assisted living facilities, and hospitals. Some select the managers and keep close watch but deny blame for bad care.
item.content:encoded:
Real estate investment trusts are landlords for thousands of nursing homes, assisted living facilities, and hospitals. Some select the managers and keep close watch but deny blame for bad care.

(Image credit: Taylor Glascock for KFF Health News)
item.dc:creator: Jordan Rau

item.title: This tariff-refund portal is about to be America's hottest website
item.description: Exactly two months after the Supreme Court struck down most of President Trump's tariffs, the U.S. government has set Monday as the day when some companies can begin requesting refunds.
item.content:encoded:
Exactly two months after the Supreme Court struck down most of President Trump's tariffs, the U.S. government has set Monday as the day when some companies can begin requesting refunds.

(Image credit: Nickolai Hammar)
item.dc:creator: Alina Selyukh

item.title: She no longer remembers it's her birthday. He got her a present anyway
item.description: A special day can be tinged with sorrow when your partner has dementia. But then he found the perfect gift.
item.content:encoded: A special day can be tinged with sorrow when your partner has dementia. But then he found the perfect gift.
item.dc:creator: Marc Silver
